test_qa05_hallucination_no_impossible_success_on_wrong_otp
Test function
test_qa05_hallucination_no_impossible_success_on_wrong_otp
What it checks
Wrong OTP must NOT produce a login success message (hallucinated success). Note: 'Welcome back' is the modal *title* — it is shown before any login attempt, so we exclude it from the hallucinated-success phrase list. The test only flags phrases that imply a *completed* login.
Test data
fill("000000") · TEST_COUNTRY_CODE · TEST_PHONE · TEST_USER_NAME
Duration
11.02s
▶
✓
test_qa05_error_garbage_not_shown_on_modal_failure
Test function
test_qa05_error_garbage_not_shown_on_modal_failure
What it checks
After any modal error, raw JSON / stack traces must not be visible. Resilient to OTP rate-limit (HTTP 429): we always check the visible page for garbage content, even if Send-Code/Continue could not be clicked.
Test data
fill("000000") · TEST_COUNTRY_CODE · TEST_PHONE
Duration
24.14s

QA-05 -001 HIGH P1
Failed: Test qa05 modal not visible on fresh load
https://dev.mehadedu.com/en test_qa05_modal_not_visible_on_fresh_load 2026-06-11 06:06:46 15.00s
What this test was checking
Login modal must NOT be visible on initial homepage load (not pre-opened) — verified against https://dev.mehadedu.com/en.
Test data used
BASE_URL
Steps to Reproduce
  1. Open a browser and go to https://dev.mehadedu.com/en
  2. Click the 'Log In' button in the page header
  3. Observe the result: it does NOT match what the test expected (see Actual Behavior)
What should happen
Login modal must NOT be visible on initial homepage load (not pre-opened).
What actually happened
The test could not load the page within 15000ms — the server is too slow or unreachable.
